bio: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nai.
bis: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Bislama.

boil: noun. A localized accumulation of pus in the skin, resulting from infection.
bos: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Bosnian.
boy: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Bodo (Central Africa).
bys: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Burak.
cibol: noun. A perennial onion plant, Allium fistulosum, commonly called Welsh onion.
cis: symbol. The function c i s (x) = cos ⁡ x + i sin ⁡ x {\displaystyle \mathrm {cis} (x)=\cos x+i\sin x}.
climb: verb. To traverse a slope or contour, either upwards (ascending) or downwards (descending).
climbs: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of climb
clomb: verb. simple past and past participle of climb
cloy: verb. To fill up or choke up; to stop up.
cloys: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of cloy
cob: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chicomuceltec.
coil: noun. Something wound in the form of a helix or spiral.
col: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Columbia-Wenatchi.
coly: noun. Any bird of either of the genera Colius or Urocolius, endemic to sub-Saharan Africa.
comb: noun. A toothed implement: A toothed implement for grooming the hair or (formerly) for keeping it in place.
cosy: adjective. Affording comfort and warmth; snug; social and comfortable.
coy: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Coyaima.
icy: adjective. Pertaining to, resembling, or abounding in ice; cold; frosty.
ism: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Masimasi.
lib: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Likum.
libs: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of lib
limb: noun. A major appendage of human or animal, used for locomotion (such as an arm, leg or wing).
limbo: noun. A speculative place or state, thought possibly to be on the edge of the bottomless pit of Hell, where the souls of innocent deceased
limby: adjective. Having many limbs, or branches.
lis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lisu.
lob: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lobi.
mib: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Atatláhuca Mixtec.
mil: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Peñoles Mixtec.
mis: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for unencoded languages.

mob: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Moinba.
moc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mocoví.
moil: verb. To toil, to work hard.

obi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Obispeño.
oily: adjective. Covered with or containing oil.
osmic: adjective. Pertaining to, derived from, or containing, osmium; specifically, designating those compounds in which it has a higher valence.

sibyl: noun. A pagan female oracle or prophetess, especially the Cumaean sibyl.

sol: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Solos.
soy: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Miyobe.

symbol: noun. A character or glyph representing an idea, concept or object.
